---
title: "Open a pull request"
description: "Get your change reviewed, merged and released"
order: 70
---

Changes reach OpenPipeline through pull requests against the **`main`** branch of
the package repository (see
[Versioning and releases](project-structure.qmd#sec-versioning)).

## The flow

1. Work on a branch in your fork (see [Set up your environment](getting-started.qmd)).
2. Make your change and [test it locally](testing.qmd).
3. Add an entry to the repository's `CHANGELOG.md` describing the change.
4. Push to your fork and open a pull request against `main`.

## Before requesting a review

Opening the pull request fills in the repository's
[PR template](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/blob/main/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md).
Work through its checklist: it asks you to self-review, link the issue you're
closing, categorize the change (breaking, new functionality, documentation, bug
fix, and so on), confirm the change is in `CHANGELOG.md`, and check that CI
passes. The CI tests run automatically on the pull request.

A maintainer reviews the pull request and, once approved, merges it into `main`.
From there [Viash Hub builds and publishes](project-structure.qmd#sec-versioning)
the package with the next release.
